options.text: This template deploys AVD image management infrastructure in the selected region. An Azure Compute Gallery is always deployed to store and version custom OS images. Optionally deploy an artifacts storage account (for image build scripts and software installers) and a build logs storage account (for customization logs, auto-purged after 7 days). Zero Trust storage hardening is applied by default: shared key access is disabled, Entra ID authentication is enforced, and network access can be restricted to private endpoints, service endpoints, or permitted IP ranges. Encryption at rest can use platform-managed keys or customer-managed keys (including HSM-backed keys) stored in a Premium Key Vault — the same key vault and rotation policy apply to both storage accounts and gallery image versions.

Optionally deploy an artifacts storage account (for image build scripts and installers) and/or a build logs storage account (for image customization logs, auto-purged after 7 days). A shared managed identity is deployed when either account is enabled. Shared key access is disabled on all accounts — Entra ID authentication is enforced.

elements.name: deployArtifactsStorageAccount
elements.label: Deploy Artifacts Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Deploy the artifacts storage account and managed identity. The managed identity is granted Storage Blob Data Reader. Disable only when all image customizations use fully-qualified public URIs or an external storage account.

elements.name: deployBuildLogsStorageAccount
elements.label: Deploy Build Logs Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Deploy a dedicated storage account for image build logs. Blobs are automatically purged after 7 days. The managed identity is granted Storage Blob Data Contributor. Pass the buildLogsStorageAccountResourceId output to imageBuild deployments as existingLogStorageAccountResourceId.

elements.name: storageNetworkAccess
elements.label: Network Access
elements.toolTip: Network access configuration applied to both storage accounts. Private Endpoint disables public access and routes traffic over the Microsoft backbone. Service Endpoint restricts access to specific subnets — this template configures the storage network ACL rules but does NOT deploy service endpoints to the subnets.

options.text: Permitted IP Addresses / CIDR Ranges (optional)

Restrict public access to specific IP addresses or CIDR ranges. Leave empty to allow all public traffic (or fully disable it via Private Endpoint with no IPs). When Private Endpoint is selected alongside IP rules, the private endpoint handles internal traffic while those IPs are permitted over the public endpoint. Has no effect on service endpoint subnet rules.

elements.name: storagePermittedIPs
elements.label: Permitted IP Addresses / CIDR Ranges
elements.ariaLabel: Permitted IP Addresses or CIDR Ranges

validations.message: Must be a valid IPv4 address or CIDR range (e.g. 10.0.0.1 or 10.0.0.0/24).

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network containing the private endpoint subnet. Only virtual networks in the primary region are shown.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('storageAccounts').privateEndpointSection.virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: privateEndpointSubnet
elements.label: Private Endpoint Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select the subnet for storage account private endpoints. Subnets with delegations are excluded.

elements.name: enablePrivateDNSIntegration
elements.label: Enable Private DNS Zone Integration
elements.toolTip: Register storage private endpoint A records in an Azure Private DNS Zone for proper name resolution.

elements.name: privateDNSZonesSubscription
elements.label: Private DNS Zone Subscription
elements.toolTip: Select the subscription containing your Private DNS Zones. In hub-and-spoke topologies this is typically the connectivity subscription.

elements.name: blobPrivateDnsZone
elements.label: Blob Storage Private DNS Zone
elements.toolTip: Select the Private DNS Zone for Blob storage (privatelink.blob.core.windows.net or privatelink.blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net for Azure Government).

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('storageAccounts').privateDnsSection.privateDNSZonesApi.value, (zone) => contains(zone.name, 'blob')), (zone) => parse(concat('{"label":"', zone.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(zone.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', zone.id, '"}')))]

options.text: This template adds the selected subnets to the storage account network ACL to allow access via service endpoints. It does NOT deploy service endpoints to the subnets. Microsoft.Storage service endpoints must already be configured on each subnet. Only subnets with an existing Microsoft.Storage service endpoint are listed below.

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network containing the subnets that will access storage via service endpoints.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('storageAccounts').serviceEndpointSection.virtualNetworksApi.value, (vnet) => equals(vnet.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (vnet) => parse(concat('{"label":"', vnet.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(vnet.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":{"name":"', vnet.name, '","id":"', vnet.id, '"}}')))]

elements.name: serviceEndpointSubnets
elements.label: Subnets with Microsoft.Storage Service Endpoint
elements.toolTip: Select all subnets that need access to the storage accounts via service endpoint. Only subnets with an existing Microsoft.Storage service endpoint are listed.

options.text: Storage accounts are encrypted at rest by default. Platform-managed keys require no additional configuration. Customer-managed keys use keys you own in a Premium Key Vault; the same vault and rotation policy are applied to both storage accounts and gallery image versions. Select HSM-backed if your Key Vault is an Azure Managed HSM or a Premium vault with HSM-protected keys.

elements.name: keyManagement
elements.label: Key Management
elements.toolTip: Encryption key management applied to all resources in this deployment — storage accounts and gallery image versions. Platform-managed keys (default) require no additional configuration. Customer-managed keys use keys you own in a Premium Key Vault; the same vault and rotation policy are applied to both storage accounts and gallery image versions. Select HSM-backed if your Key Vault is an Azure Managed HSM or a Premium vault with HSM-protected keys.

options.text: Gallery image versions are encrypted at rest by default. Platform-managed keys require no additional configuration. Customer-managed keys use keys you own in a Premium Key Vault; the same vault and rotation policy are applied to both storage accounts and gallery image versions. Select HSM-backed if your Key Vault is an Azure Managed HSM or a Premium vault with HSM-protected keys.

elements.name: keyManagement
elements.label: Key Management
elements.toolTip: Platform-managed keys (default) require no additional configuration. Customer-managed keys use keys you own in a Premium Key Vault; the same vault and rotation policy are applied to both storage accounts and gallery image versions. Select HSM-backed if your Key Vault is an Azure Managed HSM or a Premium vault with HSM-protected keys.

allowedValues.label: Platform-Managed and Customer-Managed Keys, HSM-backed (Double Encryption)

options.text: Customer-managed keys require an existing Premium SKU Key Vault with soft delete and purge protection enabled. Deploy the Key Vault using the keyVaults template before running this deployment. The deploying identity requires the Key Vault Crypto Officer role on the vault. The same vault and key rotation policy apply to both storage accounts and gallery image versions.

elements.name: encryptionKeyVault
elements.label: Encryption Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Select the Premium SKU Key Vault that holds the customer-managed encryption keys. Only Premium SKU vaults are listed. The same vault is used for storage account and gallery image version encryption.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('encryption').keyVaultSection.keyVaultsApi.value, (kv) => equals(kv.properties.sku.name, 'premium')), (kv) => parse(concat('{"label":"', kv.name, '","description":"Resource Group: ', first(skip(split(kv.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', kv.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: keyExpirationInDays
elements.label: Key Rotation (Days)
elements.toolTip: Number of days before the customer-managed encryption key expires and auto-rotates. Applies to all encrypted resources in this deployment.

elements.name: cvmDesSection
elements.label: Confidential VM Gallery Disk Encryption Set

options.text: A Confidential VM Disk Encryption Set (ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey) is required to encrypt gallery image versions that will be used to deploy ConfidentialVM session hosts with customer-managed key encryption of the VM Guest State. This option requires an HSM-backed key management selection. The CVM Orchestrator enterprise application (app ID: bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0) must be created in your tenant before proceeding.

IMPORTANT: The Confidential VM encryption key release policy is immutable once set. This deployment will fail on subsequent runs if the key already exists. Enable this option only on the first deployment per region. Disable it on all future redeployments.

elements.name: createConfidentialVmGalleryDes
elements.label: Create Confidential VM Gallery Disk Encryption Set
elements.toolTip: Creates a second DES of type ConfidentialVmEncryptedWithCustomerKey for encrypting gallery image versions intended for ConfidentialVM session host deployments. First deployment only — see warning above.

elements.name: confidentialVMOrchestratorObjectId
elements.label: CVM Orchestrator Object ID
elements.toolTip: Object ID of the Confidential VM Orchestrator enterprise application in your tenant. Run: Get-AzADServicePrincipal -ApplicationId 'bf7b6499-ff71-4aa2-97a4-f372087be7f0' | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Id

options.text: Pre-creates a stable, persistent resource group dedicated to image builds and grants the imageManagement managed identity Contributor on it. The primary purpose is delegation: image build operators who do not have subscription-level permissions to create resource groups can run image builds by pointing imageBuild to this pre-created resource group. Pass the imageBuildResourceGroupResourceId output from this deployment directly to imageBuild as imageBuildResourceGroupId.

elements.name: deployImageBuildResourceGroup
elements.label: Pre-create Image Build Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Creates a persistent resource group for image builds and grants the managed identity Contributor on it. Enables image build operators who lack subscription-level resource group creation rights to run image builds. Disable only if all image build operators have sufficient permissions to create resource groups at the subscription level.

elements.name: customImageBuildResourceGroupName
elements.label: Custom Resource Group Name
elements.toolTip: Optional. Override the auto-generated resource group name. Leave empty to use the standard naming convention. Pass the imageBuildResourceGroupResourceId output directly to imageBuild as imageBuildResourceGroupId.

validations.message: Resource group names may contain letters, numbers, underscores, parentheses, hyphens, and periods, up to 90 characters.

options.text: Define your naming convention using the components below. The defaults follow the Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) naming convention: {resource-type}-avd-{identifier}-{region}. Adjust the component order, separator, workload value, or abbreviations to match your organization's standard.

Select the components that make up your naming convention and arrange them in order using Component 1 through Component 6. Select 'None (stop here)' to end the sequence. Auto-resolved components — Resource Type, Purpose, Location — are substituted per resource at deployment time. Value components — Workload, Environment, Free-form Text 1, Free-form Text 2 — require you to supply a value in the fields that appear below.

Recommended components for conflict-free, identifiable names: Required - Resource Type (identifies the resource category: rg, gal, sa, uai, pe, nic, des), Purpose (distinguishes resources of the same type from one another), Workload (scopes names to your application or project). Highly Recommended - Location (prevents name collisions when deploying across regions).

elements.name: delimiter
elements.label: Delimiter
elements.toolTip: Character(s) placed between each component. A hyphen (-) is most common. Storage account names cannot contain hyphens — they are stripped automatically for those resources.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 3 characters: hyphens (-), underscores (_), or dots (.) only.

elements.name: component1
elements.label: Component 1
elements.toolTip: First component of every resource name.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text 1
allowedValues.description: A fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

allowedValues.label: Environment
allowedValues.description: Deployment environment tier — provide a value below (e.g. pr, np, co, dev).

allowedValues.label: Resource Type
allowedValues.description: Resource type abbreviation — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. rg, gal, uai). Optionally customize abbreviations below.

allowedValues.label: Workload
allowedValues.description: User-supplied workload/application/project name — provide a value below (e.g. avd, sharepoint).

allowedValues.label: Purpose
allowedValues.description: Resource purpose — auto-resolved per resource (e.g. image-management, assets, logs). No value required.

allowedValues.label: Location
allowedValues.description: Region abbreviation — auto-resolved from the deployment location (e.g. eus, va). Optionally override below.

allowedValues.label: Free-form Text 2
allowedValues.description: A second fixed string you define — provide a value below. The same text appears in every resource name.

elements.name: component2
elements.label: Component 2
elements.toolTip: Second component. Select 'None (stop here)' to use only 1 component.

allowedValues.label: None (stop here)
allowedValues.description: End the convention after Component 1.

elements.name: workloadValue

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 20 lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens.

elements.name: freeform1Value
elements.label: Free-form Text 1 Value
elements.toolTip: The fixed value used for the Free-form Text 1 component in all resource names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 20 lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens.

elements.name: environmentValue
elements.label: Environment Value
elements.placeholder: e.g. pr, np, co, dev, prod
elements.toolTip: The fixed value used for the Environment component in all resource names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 4 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: freeform2Value
elements.label: Free-form Text 2 Value
elements.toolTip: A second fixed text string embedded in every resource name when the Free-form Text 2 component is used.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 20 lowercase letters, digits, or hyphens.

elements.name: locationAbbreviationOverride
elements.label: Custom Location Abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Override the auto-resolved region abbreviation. Leave empty to use the standard abbreviation from the locations data file (e.g. eus for East US, va for US Gov Virginia).

constraints.validationMessage: Use up to 10 lowercase letters or digits. Leave empty to use the auto-resolved abbreviation.

options.text: No Resource Type component selected. Including Resource Type is strongly recommended - it identifies the resource category at a glance and follows the Cloud Adoption Framework naming standard (e.g. rg-avd-image-management-eus, gal-avd-imagegallery-eus).
No 'Purpose' component selected. Resources of the same type in this deployment (e.g. both storage accounts for artifacts and logs) will have identical names. Including 'Purpose' is required.

No 'Workload' component selected. Including Workload is strongly recommended - it scopes resource names to your application or project and prevents collisions with other deployments using the same convention.

No 'Location' component selected. If you plan to deploy Image Management in multiple regions (e.g. a primary gallery and a remote gallery in a second region), include Location in your convention to avoid resource name conflicts between deployments.

elements.name: rtCodeRg
elements.label: Resource Groups abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in resource group names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeGal
elements.label: Compute Galleries abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in gallery names. Gallery names replace hyphens with underscores automatically.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeUai
elements.label: User Assigned Identities abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in managed identity names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeSa
elements.label: Storage Accounts abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in storage account names. Storage names strip hyphens and are capped at 24 characters.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodePe
elements.label: Private Endpoints abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in private endpoint names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeNic
elements.label: Network Interfaces abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in private endpoint network interface names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

elements.name: rtCodeDes
elements.label: Disk Encryption Sets abbreviation
elements.toolTip: Abbreviation substituted for the Resource Type component in disk encryption set names.

constraints.validationMessage: Use 1 to 10 lowercase letters or digits.

{loc} is resolved from the selected region at deployment time. Gallery names replace all hyphens with underscores. Storage account names strip all delimiters (alphanumeric only) and always include a uniqueString suffix truncated to 24 characters.
Apply tags to the deployed resources. Tags are applied per resource type.
